CStrings:
+ "\n\nBelow is text from a receipt.\n\n"
+ "\n\nPlease summarise in 10 words or less what was purchased. Look at the line items of the\nreceipt. Reply with the summary inside XML tags.\n\nExample: <summary>purchase summary</summary>"
+ "\n\nWhat country is this receipt from? Look at the store name, language, address, and currency symbols. Give your best guess and a confidence level (high, medium, or low). If you are unsure, give two possible countries.\n\nExample: <country>United Kingdom (high)</country>\nExample with two options: <country>Germany (medium) or Austria (low)</country>"
+ "\n\nWhat is the date on this receipt? Reply with ONLY the date in YYYY-MM-DD format inside XML tags.\n\nExample: <date>YYYY-MM-DD</date>"
+ "\n\nWhat is the name of the store or restaurant? It is usually at the very top of the receipt. Do not say a payment card name, loyalty card or payment processor. Reply with ONLY the store name inside XML tags.\n\nExample: <merchant>Tesco</merchant>\n\nRemember: the STORE NAME, not a card brand like Visa or Amex."
+ "\n\nWhat is the total amount and currency? Look for the line that says Total, Grand Total or Amount Due. If there is a handwritten total or authorized amount (e.g. with tip included), use that instead of the printed total. Do NOT use Cash Tendered, Cash, Change Due, Total Savings, or Subtotal. Reply with the number and the 3-letter ISO currency code inside XML Tags.\n\nExample:\n<amount>38.02</amount>\n<currency>GBP</currency>"
